root_text.ud5.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud5.1:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena rājā pasenadi kosalo mallikāya deviyā saddhiṁ uparipāsādavaragato hoti.
root_text.ud5.1:1.4: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo mallikaṁ deviṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud5.1:1.5: “atthi nu kho te, mallike, kocañño attanā piyataro”ti?
root_text.ud5.1:2.1: “Natthi kho me, mahārāja, kocañño attanā piyataro.
root_text.ud5.1:2.2: Tuyhaṁ pana, mahārāja, atthañño koci attanā piyataro”ti?
root_text.ud5.1:2.3: “Mayhampi kho, mallike, natthañño koci attanā piyataro”ti.
root_text.ud5.1:3.1: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo pāsādā orohitvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho rājā pasenadi kosalo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud5.1:4.1: “Idhāhaṁ, bhante, mallikāya deviyā saddhiṁ uparipāsādavaragato mallikaṁ deviṁ etadavocaṁ:
root_text.ud5.1:4.2: ‘atthi nu kho te, mallike, kocañño attanā piyataro’ti?
root_text.ud5.1:4.3: Evaṁ vutte, mallikā devī maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud5.1:4.4: ‘natthi kho me, mahārāja, kocañño attanā piyataro.
root_text.ud5.1:4.5: Tuyhaṁ pana, mahārāja, atthañño koci attanā piyataro’ti?
root_text.ud5.1:4.6: Evaṁ vutte, ahaṁ, bhante, mallikaṁ deviṁ etadavocaṁ:
root_text.ud5.1:4.7: ‘mayhampi kho, mallike, natthañño koci attanā piyataro’”ti.
root_text.ud5.1:5.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud5.1:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud5.1:1.3: Now at that time King Pasenadi of Kosala was upstairs in the royal longhouse together with Queen Mallikā.
translation_text.ud5.1:1.4: Then King Pasenadi addressed Queen Mallikā,
translation_text.ud5.1:1.5: “Mallikā, is there anyone more dear to you than yourself?”
translation_text.ud5.1:2.2: But is there anyone more dear to you than yourself?”
translation_text.ud5.1:3.1: Then King Pasenadi of Kosala came downstairs from the stilt longhouse, went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.
translation_text.ud5.1:5.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud5.1:6.1: “Having explored every quarter with the mind,
translation_text.ud5.1:6.2: one finds no-one dearer than oneself.
translation_text.ud5.1:6.3: Likewise for others, each holds themselves dear;
translation_text.ud5.1:6.4: so one who desires self-knowledge <j>would harm no other.”
